growth_records.medium: Mineral medium (ammonium mineral salts with phosphate-carbonate buffer)

up.method: reported specific ammonia oxidation rate qNH3 (days 9-11, 3-day sampling)

sec.method: calculated = qNH3 x stoichiometry; NH3->NO2- was stoichiometric (N balance 61.0 mmol/L ~= 60.1 mmol/L NO2-), so qNO2- ~= qNH3 = 24.73

yields.Yxs: 0.40 g DCW per mol NH3 (3-day, days 9-11); 0.42 g DCW/mol NH3 for 10-day period (days 7-16)

growth_records.cite: Sedlacek et al. Transcriptomic Response of Nitrosomonas europaea Transitioned from Ammonia- to Oxygen-Limited Steady-State Growth. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00562-19. [PMC6967387]
growth_records.snippet: On days 9, 10, and 11...N. europaea consumed NH3 at a rate (qNH3) of 24.73 +/- 0.53 mmol g (dry cell weight)-1 h-1 with an apparent growth yield (Y) of 0.40 +/- 0.01 g (dry cell weight) mol-1 NH3. dilution rate/specific growth rate (mu) of
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state so mu = D = 0.01 /h (reported directly; doubling time ~70 h, consistent with ln2/0.01 = 69.3 h). qNH3 reported directly = 24.73 mmol/gDCW/h (3-day, days 9-11) or 24.04 mmol/gDCW/h (10-day period, Table 1). O2 uptake not quantified. Nitrite secretion computed from stoichiometric NH3->NO2- conversion (N balance = 61.0 +/-1.7 mmol/L vs NO2- 60.1 +/-1.4 mmol/L, ~98% recovery), giving qNO2- ~= qNH3 = 24.73 mmol/gDCW/h. Yxs reported = 0.40 (3-day)/0.42 (10-day) g DCW per mol NH3. Feed = 60 mmol/L NH4+ x 0.24 L/day = 14.4 mmol NH4+/day.

growth_records.medium: Mineral medium (ammonium mineral salts with phosphate-carbonate buffer)

up.method: reported specific ammonia oxidation rate qNH3 (days 28-30, 3-day sampling)

sec.method: calculated: only 77.5% of oxidized NH4+ recovered as NO2-, so qNO2- = 0.775 x qNH3 = 0.775 x 28.51 = 22.1
calculated by N-balance difference: 22.5% of qNH3 lost as N gas = 0.225 x 28.51 = 6.4 mmol N/gDCW/h; individual gas fluxes not resolved in this study

yields.Yxs: 0.35 g DCW per mol NH3 (3-day, days 28-30); 0.38 g DCW/mol NH3 for 10-day period (days 23-32)

growth_records.cite: Sedlacek et al. Transcriptomic Response of Nitrosomonas europaea Transitioned from Ammonia- to Oxygen-Limited Steady-State Growth. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00562-19. [PMC6967387]
growth_records.snippet: During days sampled for O2-limited growth transcriptomes (days 28, 29, and 30), the qNH3 was significantly higher (28.51 +/- 1.13 mmol g [dry cell weight]-1 h-1; P <= 0.05), while Y was significantly lower (0.35 +/- 0.01 g [dry cell weight]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state, mu = D = 0.01 /h kept constant across both conditions (stated: 'specific growth rate...kept constant during both NH3- and O2-limited growth'); td ~70 h. qNH3 reported = 28.51 mmol/gDCW/h (3-day, days 28-30) or 26.44 (10-day, Table 1). NH4+ consumption non-stoichiometric under O2 limitation: 51.8% of supplied NH4+ consumed (31.1 mmol/L), of which only 77.5% appeared as NO2- (24.1 mmol/L). qNO2- computed = 0.775 x 28.51 = 22.1 mmol/gDCW/h. Remaining 22.5% lost as NH2OH/NO/N2O -> 0.225 x 28.51 = 6.4 mmol N/gDCW/h (aggregate; individual NO/N2O fluxes not measured here). Yxs reported 0.35 (3-day)/0.38 (10-day). O2 uptake rate not quantified.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts nitrifier medium (chemostat feed, as previously described, ref 56)
growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrosomonas europaea (ATCC 19718)... were routinely cultivated at 30C in batch and chemostat culture as previously described. Chemostat cultures were maintained in steady state at a dilution rate of 0.015 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state: mu = D = 0.015 h-1 (verbatim dilution rate). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.015 = 46.2 h. Temperature 30C stated. Chemostat feed medium composition not given in this paper (cited to ref 56) -> composition [] / stated false. pH of chemostat not stated (batch experimental medium was pH 7.8, but that is a separate condition). [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

Two-organism coculture; culture_mode set to chemostat (continuous) with co-culture noted in culture_detail. Chemostat feed medium cited to ref 56 -> composition [] / stated false. growth_records.medium: Experimental batch mineral salts medium (N. europaea, ammonium)

up.method: initial 5 mM NH4+ (from 2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4) fully oxidized over the 10 h experiment; time course in Fig 1A, no numeric specific rate stated

sec.method: measured; 83.5 +/- 3.1 uM after 1 h then consumed by 4 h (concentration, not specific rate)
measured headspace peak 30.2 +/- 5.7 ppm during active NH3 oxidation
model simulation peak ~1 umol h-1 per 5 ml culture = 1 umol/h / 0.005 L = 0.2 mmol/L/h (dFBA-predicted maximum, not measured net rate)

growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental batch mineral salts medium for N. europaea contained 2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4, 0.75 mM MgSO4, 0.1 mM CaCl2, 12.5 mM KH2PO4, 1.25 mM NaH2PO4.H2O, 2.3 mM Na2CO3, and trace elements... pH... adjusted to 7.8.
growth_records.notes: Batch medium composition fully stated (verbatim, above). Conditions: 30C, pH 7.8, 200 rpm, closed batch 10 h, OD600 0.2 inoculum, aerobic (sufficient O2 in headspace). No specific growth rate reported for the batch (OD600 measured but no slope/mu given) -> growth_rate null. Uptake substrate NH4+: initial 5 mM NH4+ (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4 x2), consumed within experiment; numeric specific qNH4 not stated (time course only in Fig 1A). Secretion values are measured concentrations/peaks (NH2OH 83.5 uM at 1 h; NOx peak 30.2 ppm; N2O peak 42.1 ppm). NO 0.2 mmol/L/h derived from model-predicted max 1 umol/h per 5 ml (=0.005 L): 1e-3 mmol / 0.005 L / h = 0.2 mmol/L/h; this is a dFBA-simulated maximum, not a measured net flux. No gDW conversion available (OD only) so specific per-gDW rates not derivable.

growth_records.strain: N. europaea ATCC 19718 + N. winogradskyi Nb-255
growth_records.medium: Experimental batch mineral salts medium (ammonium, coculture)

up.method: initial 5 mM NH4+ (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4), consumed more rapidly than in N. europaea single culture (Fig 3A); no numeric specific rate stated
NO2- produced by N. europaea then oxidized by N. winogradskyi; ~8 h to consume accumulated NO2- (Fig 3A)

sec.method: final nitrification product (Fig 3A time course); no numeric specific rate stated
measured headspace peak 53.7 +/- 2.2 ppm (greater than sum of single cultures, P<0.005)
measured 54.4 +/- 7.5 uM after 1 h then consumed by 4 h

growth_records.cite: Mellbye et al. Genome-Scale, Constraint-Based Modeling of Nitrogen Oxide Fluxes during Coculture of Nitrosomonas europaea and Nitrobacter winogradskyi. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00170-17. [PMC5864417]
growth_records.snippet: N. europaea, N. winogradskyi, and coculture experimental cultures were suspended to an optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of 0.2, 0.05, and 0.25, respectively... The coculture... produced approximately 53.7 +/- 2.2 and 47.8 +/- 4.1 ppm of NO
growth_records.notes: Coculture batch on N. europaea ammonium medium (verbatim recipe). Combined inoculum OD600 0.25, 30C, pH 7.8, 200 rpm, closed batch 10 h, aerobic (transient microaerobic from O2 competition). No mu reported for batch -> null. Substrate NH4+ 5 mM initial (2.5 mM (NH4)2SO4), NO2- an intermediate. Secretion peaks measured: NOx 53.7 ppm, N2O 47.8 ppm, NH2OH 54.4 uM at 1 h. All are measured concentrations/peaks, not specific per-gDW rates; no gDW conversion available. culture_mode = co-culture (two organisms in one batch vessel).
